disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A hard, very tender lump appeared suddenly this morning at the anal verge immediately after straining, and the patient can no longer sit
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: A hard, agonizing lump came up near my back passage this morning right after I strained on the toilet, and now I can't sit down at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Background of intermittent painless bright red rectal bleeding seen as streaks on toilet paper over the past year
trigger_question_examples: Any problems with your bowels or back passage before this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: On and off over the last year I've seen streaks of bright red blood on the toilet paper, but it was never painful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chronic itch around the anus, a sensation of incomplete emptying, and tissue that occasionally prolapses on defecation requiring manual reduction
trigger_question_examples: Do you get any itching or a feeling of not fully emptying?
Does anything come down or bulge when you go to the toilet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, it gets itchy, I often feel like I haven't fully emptied, and sometimes a bit of tissue comes down when I go and I have to push it back in.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, no chills or rigors, and no purulent or foul-smelling discharge from the lump
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, chills or shivering?
Is there any pus or discharge coming from it?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't had any fever or shivers, and there's no pus or discharge coming out of it.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I don't think so — I haven't really checked my temperature but I don't feel hot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The patient is still passing urine normally and has no difficulty or inability to urinate
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to pass urine alright?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I'm passing urine fine, no trouble there.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Onset was immediately after heavy lifting and straining during a bout of constipation
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when it came on?
Have you been constipated or straining?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been constipated and straining, and it came up right after I'd been lifting something heavy and pushing on the toilet.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Simple over-the-counter analgesia (paracetamol) has given zero relief of the acute pain
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken anything for the pain?
Did the paracetamol help at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've taken paracetamol but it hasn't touched the pain at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spreading redness across the buttocks and no feeling of being systemically unwell
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading, or do you feel generally unwell?
Does the skin around it feel like it's getting worse or spreading?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, the redness isn't spreading and I don't feel unwell in myself — it's just the lump that hurts.
